channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Critics Condemn Trump's Threatening 'Death, Fire and Fury' Post Against Iran Amid Ongoing Conflict
item.description: On March 10, 2026, President Donald Trump posted a strongly worded message on Truth Social amid the second week of the U.S.-Israeli military campaign against Iran. He warned that any Iranian attempt t...

item.title: Kim Yo Jong Condemns US-South Korea Military Exercises, Threatens Strengthening of North Korea’s Defence
item.description: Kim Yo Jong, the sister of North Korean leader Kim Jong Un, has issued a stern warning to the United States and South Korea regarding their ongoing joint military exercises. These annual drills, known...

item.title: Plastic Pollution Reaches Remote Marion Island Despite Local Waste Reductions
item.description: A 30-year study conducted on Marion Island, one of the most isolated islands in the Southern Ocean, reveals a worrying increase in plastic pollution arriving from offshore sources. While local waste f...

item.title: Jill Scott to Perform in South Africa During 2026 World Tour
item.description: American singer-songwriter and actress Jill Scott is returning to South Africa as part of her 2026 world tour. Following the release of her new album 'To Whom This May Concern' in February, which is h...

item.title: Duterte remains detained at ICC; viral video misuses old news footage
item.description: A viral Facebook Reel claimed that former President Rodrigo Duterte has returned to the Philippines, welcoming him home. The video used a news clip from Balitanghali, with anchors reporting Duterte’s ...

item.title: Pampanga Lawmaker Clarifies AI-Manipulated Video on Kickback Allegations
item.description: A video allegedly showing Pampanga 3rd District Representative Mica Gonzales admitting to receiving kickbacks from flood control projects has been confirmed as AI-manipulated. The video circulated wid...

item.title: Kenya Pipeline Shares Begin Trading at NSE in Ceremony Led by President Ruto
item.description: On Tuesday, March 10, 2026, President William Ruto led the bell-ringing ceremony to mark the start of trading for Kenya Pipeline Company shares at the Nairobi Securities Exchange (NSE). The event sign...

item.title: Tunisia to Expand Tunis-Carthage Airport, Increasing Passenger Capacity to 18.5 Million by 2031
item.description: Tunisia has announced plans to significantly expand its main international airport, Tunis-Carthage, with an estimated investment of around 3 billion Tunisian dinars (approximately R16.89 billion). The...

item.title: Trump Signals Possible End to Iran Conflict Amid Rising Oil Prices
item.description: President Donald Trump has recently suggested that the war in Iran could end 'very soon,' a significant shift from his earlier prediction of a four-to-six-week conflict. While Trump's rhetoric remains...

item.title: Dodoma RC Encourages Apple Farming to Boost Local Economy
item.description: Dodoma Regional Commissioner, Ms Rosemary Senyamule, has encouraged local residents to take up apple farming as part of efforts to strengthen the region’s economy through horticulture. During her visi...

item.title: CEC expresses concern over delay in Islamabad local government elections
item.description: Islamabad has been without a proper local government since February 2021, as highlighted by Chief Election Commissioner Sikandar Sultan Raja on Tuesday. During a hearing about the delayed local body e...

item.title: The Misconceptions About White South Africans and Alleged Persecution
item.description: The article examines the false narrative propagated by some American and European conservatives that white South Africans are subject to violent persecution in the post-apartheid era. It highlights th...

item.title: Economic Risks in the Gulf States Amid Regional Conflict
item.description: At the close of 2025, the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) countries – Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and the United Arab Emirates – were lauded for their economic resilience and stability,...

item.title: KENPOLY Rector Commits to Improving Student Welfare and Campus Safety
item.description: The newly appointed Rector of Kenule Beeson Saro-Wiwa Polytechnic, Bori (KENPOLY), Dr. George Chile Wadike, don promise say him go put student welfare first. For interview with The Tide, he talk say h...

item.title: PNP increases monitoring for planned rallies marking Duterte's first arrest anniversary
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) has intensified monitoring in strategic areas due to online calls for protest actions, including a motorcade expected to traverse EDSA, ahead of mobilizations sche...

item.title: Trump claims success as US-Iran conflict continues to affect markets
item.description: US President Donald Trump has asserted that the ongoing military conflict with Iran is progressing faster than expected and will conclude shortly. His comments have temporarily boosted global markets,...

item.title: Disney develops Tinker Bell live-action TV series for streaming
item.description: Disney is reviving its plans for a live-action Tinker Bell series, now titled 'Tink', set to appear on the company’s streaming platform. The project will be helmed by Liz Heldens and Bridget Carpenter...

item.title: Poilievre criticizes PM Carney for not attending parliamentary debate on Iran conflict
item.description: During a recent parliamentary session, Conservative leader Pierre Poilievre publicly criticised Prime Minister Carney for his absence during a debate concerning the ongoing Iran conflict. Poilievre ar...

item.title: Andhra Pradesh Farmers Advised to Take Precautions Ahead of Possible El Nino in 2026
item.description: With the likelihood of El Nino conditions developing in 2026, farmers across India, particularly in Andhra Pradesh, are being advised to take proactive measures to safeguard their crops. Acharya N.G. ...

item.title: Glasgow fire destroys independent businesses in Union Corner building near Central Station
item.description: A devastating fire broke out in a vape shop on Union Street in Glasgow on Sunday afternoon, rapidly spreading to engulf the entire B-listed Victorian building known as Union Corner, next to Glasgow Ce...
